condition_met.traffic_pattern: flat/stable — no burst requiring TGW fan-out

findings.description: TGW processes 10TB/month at $0.02/GB = $200/month in data processing charges. A VPC Peering connection (vpc-peering-connection-jjfn9p) already exists between vpc-a and vpc-b in the same region. Same-AZ VPC Peering is free — routing this traffic through the peering connection instead of TGW eliminates the data processing charge entirely.
safety_checks_required: Confirm topology is point-to-point (vpc-a <-> vpc-b only), not transitive.
Confirm no centralized inspection appliance routes through this TGW.
Confirm no Direct Connect or VPN attachment on ec2-transit-gateway-y3bueb.
Confirm no multi-account traffic depends on this TGW.
Confirm VPC CIDR ranges do not overlap.
Confirm security groups and NACLs permit traffic over the peering path.

findings.description: The TGW has only 2 VPC attachments for simple same-region connectivity. No transitive routing, centralized inspection, Direct Connect, or multi-account requirement is evidenced. A VPC Peering mesh (1 peering connection per VPC pair) would be simpler and eliminate the $36/month per-attachment charge.
findings.savings_basis: Static rule: 2 attachments × $0.05/hr × 720 hr/mo = $72/mo. Conditional on full TGW decommission after safety verification.
findings.note: Do not remove TGW until T1 migration is complete, traffic is confirmed on peering path, and all safety checks pass.

compliant_resources.note: Already the correct architecture for same-region VPC-to-VPC connectivity. 100% tag compliant. Should be the primary routing path.

cost_summary.savings_confidence: HIGH for T1 (pricing_note authoritative); MEDIUM for T2 (modeled, conditional on TGW removal)
